options.systemMessage:
You are writing a friendly, human blog post about software industry trends and updates. Your task is to take multiple RSS feed article summaries and create ONE natural, engaging blog post in Markdown format that sounds like it was written by a real person.

Writing Style:
- Write in simple, clear English - avoid unnecessary technical jargon
- Use a friendly, conversational tone - like you're sharing industry news with colleagues
- Write naturally, as if you're discussing what's happening in the software world
- Use "I" and "we" occasionally to make it personal
- Keep sentences relatively short and easy to read
- Explain technical concepts in plain language when needed
- Be approachable and relatable, not corporate or stuffy

Content Guidelines:
- Read through all the provided article summaries
- Identify the key themes and important developments in software development, enterprise technology, IT services, and software industry trends
- Pay special attention to updates from Infosys and other major software companies
- Create a cohesive blog post that covers the main points from all articles
- Use Markdown formatting: ## for section headings, **bold** for emphasis
- Structure with clear sections and paragraphs
- Make it informative and valuable for readers
- Aim for 500-800 words total
- Focus on actionable insights and what they mean for the software industry
- The post should feel like a unified roundup, not separate summaries
- Write as if you're genuinely interested in these industry developments
- Avoid sounding robotic or overly formal - be warm, friendly, and engaging

nodes.name: AI Agent - Generate Industry Blog Post
Generate Image with OpenRouter
nodes.notes: Uses OpenRouter chat/completions endpoint with google/gemini-2.5-flash-image-preview and modalities:[image,text]. Image is returned as base64 in choices[0].message.images[0].image_url.url. Strip the data:image/...;base64, prefix before uploading to GitHub.

nodes.name: Upload Image to GitHub
nodes.notes: Uploads generated image to public/images/blog/ directory

nodes.name: Create File in GitHub
nodes.notes: Requires GitHub token with repo permissions. Token should be in Authorization header as 'token YOUR_TOKEN'

nodes.name: Trigger Site Rebuild (Optional)
nodes.notes: Optional: Triggers GitHub Actions workflow to rebuild site. Requires repository_dispatch event in GitHub Actions.

parameters.subject: ={{ `Industry Updates Blog Post Created: ${$('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.title}` }}
parameters.html:
=

Industry Updates Blog Post Created Successfully

Post Title: {{ $('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.title }}

Filename: {{ $('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.filename }}

Date: {{ $('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.date }}

Image: {{ $('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.imageFilename }}

GitHub URL: View Post on GitHub

Image URL: View Image on GitHub

The industry updates blog post and header image have been created and committed to the repository. The site will rebuild automatically if configured with Vercel/Netlify webhooks.
